meta_title: Impact Doors in Broadview Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Broadview Park, FL. ESW & CWI brands, $0 down financing, 170mph wind-rated. Call for a free estimate today!
h1: Impact Doors for Broadview Park Homes — Built Tough Enough for South Florida's Worst Storms
intro:
Broadview Park is a tight-knit residential community tucked between Fort Lauderdale, Plantation, and Davie — a neighborhood where working families have invested real dollars into homes averaging around $310,000. With more than half of all homes in Broadview Park built before 1994, many of those properties were constructed before today's strict hurricane-rated building standards took effect in Broward County. That means thousands of front entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors throughout the 33311 and 33312 ZIP codes may still be offering little more than minimal resistance against the kind of violent wind-driven storms that have repeatedly struck this area over the past three decades.

Broadview Park sits approximately six miles from the Atlantic coastline — close enough to feel the full force of landfalling hurricanes and tropical storms, yet beyond the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) boundary. Don't let that distinction create a false sense of security. Broward County still mandates hurricane-rated products under the Florida Building Code, and with a designated wind zone of 170 mph, every door opening in your home must be capable of withstanding powerful pressure loads and windborne debris. Impact doors are no longer a luxury upgrade for Broadview Park homeowners — they are a code-required, financially smart, and life-safety essential.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped protect hundreds of South Florida homes from the kind of destruction that unprotected doors invite. When a storm sends debris hurtling at 100+ mph, an ordinary door becomes a dangerous liability. Our impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — sourced from trusted brands ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — are engineered specifically for communities like Broadview Park, where residents deserve the same level of protection as anyone living on the barrier islands. Whether your home is steps from Broadview Park Elementary or tucked into one of the neighborhood's quiet streets near the Fort Lauderdale city limits, we bring the expertise, the products, and the craftsmanship to fortify every entry point in your home.

section_why.h2: Why Broadview Park Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home, you're trusting them with one of your most valuable assets — and in a community like Broadview Park, where average home values hover around $310,000, that trust needs to be earned. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by doing things the right way: 100% in-house installation, no subcontractors, and a team that brings over 60 years of combined experience to every project across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties.

What makes our approach different is the consistency. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same skilled technicians who sold you the project are not strangers to the crew that shows up on installation day. Our in-house team handles everything from the initial measurement and product selection to the final inspection walkthrough with the Broward County Building Division. We've completed more than 500 homes across South Florida, and our A+ BBB rating reflects the level of accountability we hold ourselves to on every single job — from the largest Plantation estate to the most modest Broadview Park bungalow.

We also understand the specific building stock of neighborhoods like Broadview Park, where more than half the homes predate the post-Hurricane Andrew code overhaul of 1994. Older door frames, non-standard rough openings, and decades of South Florida humidity can all complicate an impact door installation if a crew doesn't know what they're doing. Our technicians are trained to handle those exact scenarios — properly reinforcing frames, correcting structural issues before they become post-installation problems, and ensuring a watertight, airtight seal that performs as well on day 3,000 as it did on day one.

Financing is another area where Premier Impact Windows & Roofing sets itself apart from the competition. We believe that hurricane protection shouldn't be out of reach for working families, which is why we offer payment options starting from just $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified buyers. Protecting your Broadview Park home is an investment in safety, resale value, and long-term savings — and we make sure the financial side of that decision is as stress-free as possible.

section_history.h2: Broadview Park's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Doors Every Time
section_history.body:
Broadview Park has faced some of the most destructive storms to strike the continental United States in modern memory. Since 1990, the area has experienced the effects of nine named storms, with three standing out as watershed events that permanently changed how Broward County homeowners think about hurricane preparedness. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains one of the most catastrophic natural disasters in U.S. history — while its epicenter was in Miami-Dade County, the storm's outer bands still delivered damaging winds and debris to Broward communities. Andrew's destruction was a wake-up call that ultimately led to a sweeping overhaul of Florida's building codes, but homes already standing in Broadview Park at the time were grandfathered in — and many still stand today with their original, pre-code doors.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit Broward County with particular ferocity, making landfall on the southwest coast before racing across the peninsula and striking the Fort Lauderdale metro area with Category 2 conditions. Broadview Park residents experienced sustained winds, widespread power outages lasting weeks, and the kind of projectile debris that shatters unprotected doors and windows in seconds. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, a massive Category 4 storm that tracked directly up the spine of Florida. While Irma weakened slightly before impacting Broward County, it still produced dangerous wind gusts throughout the 33311 and 33312 ZIP codes and caused significant property damage to homes without impact-rated protection.

The pattern is clear: Broadview Park is not immune to hurricane-force conditions, and the consequences of under-protected doors can range from costly water intrusion and structural damage to complete loss of the door envelope — creating a pressure differential that can literally blow the roof off a home. Impact doors break that cycle by maintaining the building envelope under extreme conditions, protecting both life and property when the storms that South Florida's history guarantees will come again.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Requirements for Impact Doors in Broadview Park
section_code.body:
Broadview Park falls outside the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which means it is not subject to the most stringent Miami-Dade product approval standards required in coastal areas. However, this does not exempt homeowners or contractors from significant regulatory requirements. Under the Florida Building Code (FBC), all new door installations and replacements in Broadview County must meet hurricane protection standards appropriate for the local wind speed design requirement — which, for the Broadview Park area, is a formidable 170 miles per hour. Any impact door installed in this community must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) number demonstrating it has passed the required large and small missile impact testing and cyclic pressure testing protocols.

Permit compliance in Broadview Park runs through the Broward County Building Division, which enforces the FBC and reviews product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing before issuing permits. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on behalf of our customers — we prepare and submit all required documentation, coordinate inspections, and ensure that every installation passes final approval. Trying to replace an impact door without pulling the proper permits is a serious mistake that can complicate homeowners insurance claims, trigger fines, and create significant liability issues at the time of resale.

For homeowners in the 33311 and 33312 ZIP codes, it's also worth understanding that Florida Statute 553.842 governs product approval statewide, and that the specific hardware, glazing, and frame systems used in any impact door installation must match exactly what was tested and approved. This is one reason why working with a licensed, experienced installer like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing matters so much — product substitutions or improper installation techniques can void approvals and leave your home unprotected in ways that aren't visible until a storm reveals the deficiency.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Hurricane Protection Engineered for Broadview Park Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors exclusively from two of the industry's most trusted manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned their place at the top of the South Florida market by delivering products that meet and exceed the rigorous performance standards required by the Florida Building Code — built specifically to handle the wind loads, humidity, and salt-air conditions that define this region's climate.

ESW's impact door lineup covers the full range of openings common in Broadview Park homes, including heavy-duty entry doors with reinforced steel or fiberglass construction, multi-point locking systems that engage at the top, bottom, and sides of the frame simultaneously for maximum security and pressure resistance, and sliding glass door systems engineered for smooth operation without sacrificing structural integrity. ESW products undergo stringent large-missile impact testing, cyclic wind pressure testing, and water infiltration testing — giving homeowners in the 33311 and 33312 ZIP codes confidence that their doors will perform when the stakes are highest. CWI rounds out our offering with an impressive selection of impact-resistant French doors, decorative entry doors, and patio sliders that combine architectural beauty with certified hurricane performance.

Beyond storm protection, the products we install offer meaningful day-to-day benefits that Broadview Park homeowners will notice immediately. The multi-layered laminated glass used in ESW and CWI impact doors provides outstanding noise reduction — important for homes near busy corridors connecting to Fort Lauderdale and Plantation. The thermal performance of these door systems also delivers measurable energy savings by reducing heat transfer and keeping air conditioning inside where it belongs during long South Florida summers. And because both ESW and CWI products are designed for our climate, they resist warping, swelling, and deterioration caused by decades of heat and humidity that cause conventional door systems to fail well before their time.

section_cost.h2: What Does Impact Door Installation Cost in Broadview Park — And How Does It Pay for Itself?
section_cost.body:
For Broadview Park homeowners with properties valued around $310,000, the investment in impact doors is one of the most financially defensible upgrades available. The exact cost of an impact door installation depends on factors including the number of openings, the door style (entry, sliding glass, or French door), glass specifications, and frame requirements — but homeowners can generally expect to invest anywhere from $1,500 to $4,500 per door opening for a fully permitted, professionally installed impact door system. When you consider the insurance savings, energy efficiency gains, and protection of home equity, the math works strongly in your favor.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that math even more accessible through flexible financing options designed with Broadview Park families in mind. Qualified homeowners can take advantage of payment plans starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Rather than depleting savings or delaying critical hurricane protection while you save up, our financing lets you start protecting your home now and manage the cost over time on a schedule that works with your household budget. For a lower-middle income community like Broadview Park, that kind of payment flexibility is not a minor detail — it's the difference between getting protected and remaining vulnerable.

The return on investment for impact doors goes well beyond storm protection. In Broward County, impact doors contribute to a wind mitigation inspection report, which can unlock meaningful discounts on homeowners insurance premiums — savings that in many cases amount to hundreds of dollars per year. Over a 10-to-15-year period, those annual savings can offset a significant portion of the original installation cost. Add in reduced energy bills, increased resale appeal in a competitive South Florida real estate market, and the peace of mind of knowing your family is safe during every hurricane season, and impact doors represent one of the smartest investments a Broadview Park homeowner can make.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Real Insurance Savings on Your Broadview Park Home with Certified Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling and least discussed benefits of installing impact doors in Broadview Park is the effect they can have on your homeowners insurance premium. Florida is one of the most expensive states in the nation for home insurance, and Broward County homeowners are all too familiar with rates that seem to climb every year regardless of whether they've filed a claim. What many residents don't realize is that certified hurricane-rated door and window replacements can trigger meaningful credits through the wind mitigation inspection process — credits that insurance carriers are required to apply under Florida law.

When you replace standard doors with impact-rated systems from manufacturers like ESW or CWI, and that replacement is properly permitted and inspected through the Broward County Building Division, you become eligible for a wind mitigation inspection that documents the upgraded protection on your home. The resulting report is submitted to your insurance carrier — whether that's Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Property & Casualty, or one of the many private carriers operating in Broward County — and it directly influences your premium calculation. Homeowners with full impact-rated openings (windows and doors) often see the most significant premium reductions, which can range from 15% to 45% depending on the carrier and the specific construction characteristics of the home.

For a Broadview Park homeowner currently paying $3,000 to $5,000 or more per year in homeowners insurance — a range that reflects the real-world experience of many residents in the 33311 and 33312 ZIP codes — even a 20% reduction represents $600 to $1,000 in annual savings. Those savings compound over time and can make a substantial dent in the overall cost of your impact door investment. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can walk you through what to expect from the wind mitigation process and help ensure your installation is documented and permitted in a way that maximizes the credits available to you.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Broadview Park if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Broadview Park is roughly six miles from the Atlantic coast and sits outside the HVHZ, Broward County still falls within a 170 mph design wind speed zone under the Florida Building Code. Storms like Hurricane Wilma and Hurricane Irma demonstrated that inland communities like Broadview Park experience destructive winds and windborne debris just as dangerous as coastal areas. Impact doors are required for new installations and replacements under current code, and they provide critical protection for the 55% of Broadview Park homes built before modern standards were enacted.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Broadview Park, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Broadview Park typically range from $1,500 to $4,500 per opening depending on the door type, size, and style — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. This allows Broadview Park families to protect their homes immediately without a large upfront investment, while potentially offsetting costs through insurance savings and energy bill reductions over time.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Broadview Park?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door replacements in Broadview Park require a permit through the Broward County Building Division. The permit process involves submitting product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing information before work begins, followed by a final inspection once installation is complete. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on behalf of our customers, ensuring full code compliance and a clean record that protects your home value and insurance coverage.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Broadview Park home?
faqs.a: The timeline from contract signing to completed installation typically runs four to eight weeks in Broadview Park, with the largest variable being the permitting and product lead time from our manufacturing partners ESW and CWI. Once permits are approved and products arrive, most single-door installations are completed in one day, while multi-door projects may require two to three days. Our in-house installation team handles everything start to finish, so there are no delays caused by subcontractor scheduling or miscommunication between crews.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors actually lower my homeowners insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — often significantly. After a permitted impact door installation in Broadview Park, you are eligible for a wind mitigation inspection. The resulting report documents your home's hurricane-rated openings and is submitted to your insurance carrier. Florida law requires carriers to apply wind mitigation credits, and homes with fully impact-rated doors and windows often see premium reductions of 15% to 45%. For Broward County homeowners paying $3,000 or more annually in insurance, that can translate to hundreds of dollars in savings every year.
